entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: West-African sorghum (Sorghum bicolor L. Moench), including varieties Fadda, IS15401, Soumba and 621B

entities.name: RAINFALL UNCERTAINTY
entities.description: Unpredictable rainfall patterns affecting crop production in the Sahel

entities.name: NUTRIENT DEFICIENCY
entities.description: Lack of essential nutrients in the soil affecting sorghum production

entities.name: SAHEL
entities.description: The region where sorghum production is affected by rainfall uncertainty and nutrient deficiency

entities.name: FERTILIZER
entities.description: Nutrients applied to the soil, specifically NPK and urea, to improve crop growth

entities.name: WATER REGIME
entities.description: The availability and pattern of water, including irrigated or non-irrigated conditions

entities.name: FERTILIZATION STRATEGIES
entities.description: Alternative approaches to applying fertilizer based on rainfall patterns and growing stages

entities.name: SPLIT PLOT EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: The experimental design used to test the responses of sorghum varieties to fertilizer and water

entities.name: SENEGAL
entities.description: The country where the split plot experiments were conducted

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: A growth parameter of sorghum affected by fertilizer timing and rate

entities.name: LEAF NUMBER
entities.description: A growth parameter of sorghum affected by fertilizer timing and rate

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area, affected by water, nitrogen, and variety interactions

entities.name: BIOMASS
entities.description: The total mass of the plant, affected by fertilizer timing and rate

entities.name: NIORO DU RIP
entities.description: A specific location in Senegal where experiments were carried out

entities.name: SINTHIOU MALÈME
entities.description: A specific location in Senegal where experiments were carried out

entities.name: CERES-SORGHUM MODEL
entities.description: A simulation model used to predict grain yields based on fertilization

entities.name: WEATHER
entities.description: Atmospheric conditions that should manage fertilizer application to achieve acceptable grain yield
